content.cn_text: 第三部分。你将听到两名学生，名字叫
content.en_text: Section 3. You will hear two students called

content.cn_text: Josh 和 Emily，讨论一项关于将科学技术应用于艺术的作业。
content.en_text: Josh and Emily discussing an assignment on applying scientific techniques to art.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间浏览第5页第21至第24题。
content.en_text: First you have some time to look at questions 21 to 24 on page 5.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第21至第24题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 24.

content.cn_text: Josh：嗨，Emily。 Emily：嗨，Josh，准备好做作业了吗？
content.en_text: Josh: Hi Emily. Emily: Hi Josh, are you ready to work on the assignment?

content.cn_text: Josh：准备好了。我们需要描述现代科学技术如何被用于艺术史领域。
content.en_text: Josh: Yeah. Now we need to describe how modern scientific techniques are being used in the field of art history.

content.cn_text: Emily：对。Abbott 博士建议我们选择一些著名案例来说明论点。
content.en_text: Emily: Right. And Dr Abbott suggested that we choose some famous cases to illustrate the argument.

content.cn_text: Josh：好，那你找到了什么？
content.en_text: Josh: Okay, so what have you found?

content.cn_text: Emily：有一位加拿大法医科学家叫 Biro。
content.en_text: Emily: Well, there's a Canadian forensic scientist called Biro.

content.cn_text: Josh：是的，我好像听说过他。
content.en_text: Josh: Yes, I think I've heard of him.

content.cn_text: 你对他了解什么？
content.en_text: What do you know about him?

content.cn_text: 嗯，我是 Jackson Pollock 的忠实粉丝。
content.en_text: Well, I'm a big fan of Jackson Pollock.

content.cn_text: 就是那位现代美国艺术家。
content.en_text: The modern American artist.

content.cn_text: Emily：他是不是画那些特别大的抽象画？
content.en_text: Emily: Didn't he paint those really huge abstract paintings?

content.cn_text: Josh：就是他。
content.en_text: Josh: That's him.

content.cn_text: Emily：我猜伪造那种画应该挺容易的。
content.en_text: Emily: I guess it would be pretty easy to fake one of those paintings.

content.cn_text: Josh：绝不可能，Emily。我知道很多人觉得连孩子都能画出一幅。
content.en_text: Josh: No way Emily. I know a lot of people think that even a child could paint one.

content.cn_text: 在未经训练的人眼里，它们可能看起来简单，但其实是极其复杂精细的艺术作品。
content.en_text: To the untrained eye they might look simple but they're incredibly intricate works of art.

content.cn_text: Emily：抱歉，但我真的不同意你。
content.en_text: Emily: Well sorry, but I really can't agree with you.

content.cn_text: 总之，Biro 发现了什么？
content.en_text: Anyway what did Biro find out?

content.cn_text: Josh：Biro 处理过一个案例，一位客户请他证明她只花5美元买到的一幅画是真正的 Jackson Pollock。
content.en_text: Josh: Well Biro worked on a case where a client asked him to prove that a painting she bought for only five dollars was an authentic Jackson Pollock.

content.cn_text: Emily：所以它是假的吗？
content.en_text: Emily: So was it a fake?

content.cn_text: Josh：Biro 找到了证据证明它是真正的 Pollock，但艺术界不接受他的发现。
content.en_text: Josh: Well Biro found evidence to show it was a genuine Pollock but the art world didn't accept his findings.

content.cn_text: Emily：为什么？
content.en_text: Emily: Why?

content.cn_text: Josh：一位评论家说，与其他 Pollock 作品相比，这幅画上的白色和黄色线条太直了。
content.en_text: Josh: Well one critic said that compared to other Pollocks the white and yellow lines on the painting were too straight.

content.cn_text: Emily：拜托，Josh。
content.en_text: Emily: Come on Josh.

content.cn_text: 这听起来没那么有说服力。
content.en_text: That doesn't seem that convincing.

content.cn_text: Josh：是，你说得对，这个理由有点弱。
content.en_text: Josh: No you're right, it is a bit weak.

content.cn_text: 但最有力的论点是，它没有历任持有者记录。
content.en_text: But the strongest argument was that there were no records of previous owners.

content.cn_text: 如果这幅画是真品，应该能够一路追溯到艺术家的工作室。
content.en_text: For the painting to be authentic you really should be able to trace the painting all the way back to the artist's studio.

content.cn_text: Emily：嗯，我想这倒是真的。
content.en_text: Emily: Well that's true I suppose.

content.cn_text: 他们还咨询了其他人吗？
content.en_text: Did they consult with anyone else?

content.cn_text: Josh：国际艺术研究基金会参与了。
content.en_text: Josh: The International Foundation for Art Research got involved.

content.cn_text: Emily：他们的结论是什么？
content.en_text: Emily: And what was their verdict?

content.cn_text: Josh：他们发现所使用的绘画技法有明显相似之处。
content.en_text: Josh: Well they saw a definite similarity in the painting techniques used.

content.cn_text: Emily：你是说 Pollock 把油彩滴在画布上的方式？
content.en_text: Emily: You mean the way Pollock dripped the oils on the canvas?

content.cn_text: Josh：是的，他们还注意到画背面的泥土和颜料痕迹，这些是 Pollock 的画都有的。
content.en_text: Josh: Yeah, and they noted the dirt and paint marks on the back which all of Pollock's paintings have.

content.cn_text: Emily：为什么会这样？
content.en_text: Emily: Why's that?

content.cn_text: Josh：因为他画画时通常把画布平铺在地上。
content.en_text: Josh: He used to lie his canvases down on the ground when he painted them.

content.cn_text: 但基金会担心这幅画中使用的丙烯颜料。
content.en_text: But the foundation were worried about the acrylic paint that was used on the painting.

content.cn_text: 这种颜料现在很常见，但在 Pollock 的时代非常少见。
content.en_text: It's quite common now but it was very unusual back in Pollock's day.

content.cn_text: Emily：所以它很可能是更晚才画的。
content.en_text: Emily: So it was probably painted much later.

content.cn_text: Josh：对。
content.en_text: Josh: Right.

content.cn_text: Emily：那在这个争论中，我们站哪一边？
content.en_text: Emily: So whose side are we on in this argument?

content.cn_text: Josh：谈到艺术，你能理解艺术评论家的观点。
content.en_text: Josh: When it comes to art you can see the art critics point.

content.cn_text: 如果他们的知识和专业经验告诉他们这绝对不是 Pollock 的画，
content.en_text: If their knowledge and expertise tells them that it definitely isn't a Pollock painting,

content.cn_text: 我们难道不该相信他们吗？
content.en_text: shouldn't we believe them?

content.cn_text: 我的意思是，艺术界通常就是这样运作的，不是吗？
content.en_text: I mean that is usually how it works in the art world isn't it?

content.cn_text: Emily：艺术史学家一直以来都会评判画作，但现在情况正在改变。
content.en_text: Emily: Art historians have always judged paintings but things are changing now.

content.cn_text: 如果我们有像指纹分析这样的现代科学技术，为什么不用呢？
content.en_text: If we have modern scientific techniques like fingerprint analysis, why not use them?

content.cn_text: Josh：这倒是真的。
content.en_text: Josh: That's true.

content.cn_text: 也许我们需要两者结合。
content.en_text: Well maybe we need a combination.

content.cn_text: Emily：我觉得你说得对。
content.en_text: Emily: I think you're right.

content.cn_text: 在听剩余讨论之前，你有一些时间浏览第6页第25至第30题。
content.en_text: Before you hear the rest of the discussion you have some time to look at questions 25 to 30 on page 6.

content.cn_text: 现在请听录音，并回答第25至第30题。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 25 to 30.

content.cn_text: Emily：Josh，那你还发现了什么？
content.en_text: Emily: So did you find out anything else Josh?

content.cn_text: Josh：你听说过一幅叫《安吉亚里之战》的画吗？
content.en_text: Josh: Well have you ever heard of a painting called The Battle of Anghiari?

content.cn_text: Emily：我确实听说过。
content.en_text: Emily: I have actually.

content.cn_text: 那是著名意大利艺术家 Leonardo da Vinci 的失落杰作。
content.en_text: It's the Lost Masterpiece by Leonardo da Vinci, the famous Italian artist.

content.cn_text: 它怎么了？
content.en_text: What about it?

content.cn_text: Josh：一位名叫 Seracini 的意大利艺术分析师认为他知道它在哪里，并且正在用科学技术帮助定位它。
content.en_text: Josh: Well an Italian art analyst called Seracini thinks he knows where it is and he's using scientific techniques to help locate it.

content.cn_text: Emily：怎么做？
content.en_text: Emily: How?

content.cn_text: Josh：艺术史学家知道这幅画最后一次出现在哪里。
content.en_text: Josh: Well art historians knew where the painting was last seen.

content.cn_text: 就在佛罗伦萨的五百人大厅。
content.en_text: That's right in the Hall of the 500 in Florence.

content.cn_text: Emily：对。
content.en_text: Emily: Right.

content.cn_text: Josh：所以 Seracini 提出理论，认为这幅画还在那里，并开始证明这一点。
content.en_text: Josh: So Seracini had a theory that the painting was still there and he set out to prove it.

content.cn_text: 他使用了很多不同技术，比如雷达和热成像相机。
content.en_text: He used a lot of different technology like radar and thermographic cameras.

content.cn_text: 但一开始，他用激光扫描了大厅的每一寸，以制作一个非常精确的建筑设计3D模型。
content.en_text: But initially he scanned every inch of the hall with a laser in order to make a really accurate 3D model of the building's design.

content.cn_text: Emily：他认为这幅画藏在建筑里的某个地方吗？
content.en_text: Emily: Did he think the painting was hidden in the building somewhere?

content.cn_text: Josh：正是。
content.en_text: Josh: Exactly.

content.cn_text: 然后，为了弄清大厅在 Leonardo 时代是什么样子，他用热成像相机拍摄了它。
content.en_text: Then to find out what the hall looked like in Leonardo's time, he took pictures of it with a thermographic camera.

content.cn_text: Emily：他不能用超声波来做这件事吗？
content.en_text: Emily: Couldn't he have used ultrasound to do that?

content.cn_text: Josh：不太行。
content.en_text: Josh: Not really.

content.cn_text: 不同类型的建筑材料会产生不同量的辐射，在热成像图中可以清楚看到这些差异。
content.en_text: Different types of building materials produce different amounts of radiation and you can really see those differences in a thermal image.

content.cn_text: 所以比如说，砖会和木材或玻璃看起来不同。
content.en_text: So brick would look different from wood or glass, for example.

content.cn_text: Emily：是的。
content.en_text: Emily: Yes.

content.cn_text: Josh：Seracini 弄清了这座建筑最初建成时的样子，以及后来进行了哪些改造。
content.en_text: Josh: Seracini worked out what the building looked like when it was first built and what renovations took place after that.

content.cn_text: 他发现东墙上的两扇大玻璃窗在 Da Vinci 开始作画时已经被移除并填上，留下了足够大的空间。
content.en_text: He found that two large glass windows on the east wall had been removed and filled in by the time Da Vinci started work on his painting, leaving a space big enough for it.

content.cn_text: Emily：但为什么这幅画400年来都没人见过？
content.en_text: Emily: But why hasn't the painting been seen for 400 years?

content.cn_text: Josh：为了回答这个问题，Seracini 需要更仔细地研究那面墙。
content.en_text: Josh: To answer that question, Seracini needed to study that wall even more closely.

content.cn_text: Emily：他又用了热成像相机吗？
content.en_text: Emily: Did he use the thermographic camera again?

content.cn_text: Josh：没有，这次他用了另一种技术，雷达。
content.en_text: Josh: No, this time he used another kind of technology, radar.

content.cn_text: Emily：那显示了什么？
content.en_text: Emily: And what did that show?

content.cn_text: Josh：它显示那里实际上有两堵墙，一堵旧墙，前面又建了一堵新墙。
content.en_text: Josh: It showed that there were in fact two walls there, an older one and a newer one built in front of it.

content.cn_text: Emily：所以 Seracini 认为 Da Vinci 的画在那堵被隐藏的墙上吗？
content.en_text: Emily: So does Seracini believe that Da Vinci's painting is on the concealed wall?

content.cn_text: Josh：是的。
content.en_text: Josh: Yes.

content.cn_text: 在一堵被较新的砖墙遮住的石墙上。
content.en_text: On a stone wall concealed by the newer brick wall.

content.cn_text: Emily：那调查的下一阶段是什么？
content.en_text: Emily: So what's the next stage in the investigation?

content.cn_text: Josh：他不被允许移除任何灰泥或砖块，所以现在正在尝试使用伽马射线相机来验证他的理论。
content.en_text: Josh: Well, he isn't allowed to remove any plaster or bricks, so he's now experimenting with a gamma ray camera to try and verify his theory.

content.cn_text: Emily：我觉得我们已经有了一些非常有力的例子，说明科学如何帮助艺术。
content.en_text: Emily: Well, I think we've got some really strong examples there of how science can help art.

content.cn_text: 我们看看能不能把大纲打出来。
content.en_text: Let's see if we can type up an outline.

content.cn_text: 第三部分到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of section 3.

content.cn_text: 你现在有半分钟时间检查答案。
content.en_text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.

content.cn_text: 现在请翻到第四部分。
content.en_text: Now turn to section 4.

content.cn_text: 在第3页。
content.en_text: On page 3.

content.cn_text: 现在请翻到第四部分。
content.en_text: Now turn to section 4.

content.cn_text: 在第3页。
content.en_text: On page 3.

content.cn_text: 现在请翻到第四部分。
content.en_text: Now turn to section 4.

content.cn_text: 在第3页。
content.en_text: On page 3.

content.cn_text: 现在请翻到第四部分。
content.en_text: Now turn to section 4.

content.cn_text: 在第7页。
content.en_text: On page 7.

groups.desc: Choose the correct letters, A, B, or C.
groups.title: Questions 21-24.

items.title: What does Josh think about Jackson Pollock’s paintings?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第21题问 Josh 怎么看 Jackson Pollock 的画。原文说它们看似 simple，但其实 incredibly intricate works of art，所以答案是 B. They are complex。

items.title: The $5 painting was considered to be a fake because
items.analysis: 答案解析：第22题问这幅5美元的画为什么被认为是假的。原文说 strongest argument 是 no records of previous owners，也就是缺少来源文件/记录，所以答案是 A. it lacked documentation。

items.title: What made the International Foundation for Art Research reject the $5 painting?

options.title: A
options.content: what was on the back of the painting

items.analysis: 答案解析：第23题问国际艺术研究基金会为什么拒绝这幅5美元的画。原文说他们 worried about the acrylic paint，因为这种颜料在 Pollock 时代很少见，所以答案是 B. the type of paint used。

items.title: What do Josh and Emily agree about art evaluation?

options.title: A
options.content: Only an experienced critic can evaluate a painting’s authenticity.

options.title: B
options.content: Modern scientific methods have replaced the traditional approach.

options.title: C
options.content: Experts from the science and art worlds should work together.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第24题问 Josh 和 Emily 对艺术评估达成什么一致意见。原文最后说 maybe we need a combination，并得到赞同，意思是艺术专家和科学方法应结合，所以答案是 C. Experts from the science and art worlds should work together。

items.title: Seracini used _____ to help make a model of the building.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第25题问 Seracini 用什么帮助建立建筑模型。原文说 scanned every inch with a laser to make a 3D model，对应选项 D. a laser scanner。

items.title: Seracini used _____ to reveal different materials in the walls.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第26题问 Seracini 用什么显示墙体中不同材料。原文说用 thermographic camera，并解释 thermal image 能显示材料差异，所以答案是 G. a thermographic camera。ultrasound 被否定，是干扰。

items.title: Seracini guessed that Da Vinci painted his masterpiece on the east wall, in a space that used to hold _____
items.analysis: 答案解析：第27题问 Da Vinci 作画的空间原本用来放什么。原文说 two large glass windows had been removed and filled in，所以答案是 F. glass。

items.title: Seracini analysed the wall using _____ and discovered a second wall behind it.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第28题问 Seracini 用什么分析墙并发现后面有第二堵墙。原文说 this time he used radar，所以答案是 E. a radar machine。

items.title: Seracini hypothesised that the Da Vinci painting is still there on the original _____ wall.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第29题问 Da Vinci 的画仍在原来的什么墙上。原文说 on a stone wall concealed by the newer brick wall，所以答案是 C. stone。brick 是新墙材料，不是原墙。

items.title: Seracini is using _____ to prove his theory.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第30题问 Seracini 正在用什么证明自己的理论。原文说 experimenting with a gamma ray camera，对应选项 B. gamma-ray technology。
